conversation.message: How have you been feeling lately?
Irritable. I get annoyed at the smallest things. Like if someone is talking loudly on the phone or the traffic is slow. It’s just little things, but it gets on my nerves.
Has that irritability been affecting your sleep at all?

Yeah. I lie there and think about all the things I need to do tomorrow, or I think about how I should’ve done something differently today. I can't turn my brain off. I usually just get up and watch TV until I’m too tired to stay awake.
When you're up late watching TV, do you find you're actually interested in what you're watching, or is it just something to pass the time?
It’s just something to pass the time, mostly. I don't really care about the shows, but I can't stop thinking. It’s like my brain is just running in circles.
You mentioned thinking about how you should've done things differently—does that happen a lot during the day too?
Constantly. I'll be doing something simple, like cooking, and I'll think, 'Why did I do it like that?' or 'I should've done it this way.' It's like I'm constantly second-guessing myself.
When you're second-guessing yourself like that, does it ever make you feel like you're just not measuring up?
Yeah. It makes me feel like I'm losing my grip. I used to be so sure of myself, but now I just feel like I'm just winging it.
When you feel like you're just winging it, does that change how you see your future?
I don't know what I'm going to do with my life, honestly. I used to have a plan, but now it feels like everything is just up in the air. I don't know what I'm supposed to be doing.
When you feel like everything is up in the air, does that make you feel more on edge or restless during the day?
Definitely restless. I get up and pace around the living room just to get my blood moving. I feel like I'm just waiting for something to happen, but I don't know what that is.
When you're pacing and waiting for something to happen, what kind of thoughts are going through your head?
Nothing, really. Just the sound of my own footsteps. I feel like I'm just standing still, but my body is moving.
When you're pacing and feeling like you're just standing still, does that feeling of waiting ever make it hard to get started on things you need to do?
Yeah. I'll look at a task and think, 'I should probably start now,' but I just can't bring myself to do it. I just stand there until I get too tired to move.
When you get too tired to move, what do you end up doing instead?
I just sit down. I usually just sit there for a bit until I can muster up the energy to get up and do something else.
